text:
A French pole vaulter who missed out on the final at theParis Olympicsbecause of his penis has been urged to ‘call a drag queen’ by a former tennis star.

Anthony Ammirati sent the internet into a frenzy over the weekend when his manhood clipped the 5.70-metre bar, denying him a spot in Monday’s final.

It was a bizarre moment, and now ex-professional tennis player and Serena Williams' former coach Rennae Stubbs has since weighed in on the incident.

Taking to social media to offer a tip to Ammirati,Stubbs commented: “Seriously!!! When your sport is all about getting your body over a bar and it’s a matter of millimetres!

“My man! Call a drag queen! She’ll teach u how to tuck it! This is unbelievable!”

Stubbs was referencing a practice called ‘tucking’ adopted by drag queens, which sees the male genitals tucked between the legs and the buttocks - something which would’ve certainly helped Ammirati out in his event.

Among the memes and the online jibes, Ammirati has been dealing with the disappointment of his Olympic dream coming to an end early.

In the aftermath of the weekend mishap, Ammirati broke his silence on his exit from the competition, but the Frenchman didn’t directly reference his penis-related gaffe.

Pick up your ultimate guide to the Olympics! Going for Gold provides the day-by-day rundown of what's on and when. Click here to buy.





Speaking to the French Athletics Federation, Ammirati said: “It’s a big disappointment. I’m a bit gutted, because I didn’t miss anything on the third attempt at 5.70 m.

“What I did miss was a bit of jumping in training to fine-tune the settings. Just a technical session.

He added: “I was 100 per cent physically, but I was missing a bit of pole vault.

“The conditions were good. It’s the first time I’ve started a competition without any stress.

Don't miss:Carlos Alcaraz cries and stops interview after losing Olympic gold to Djokovic

Don't miss:Team GB's Amber Rutter 'cheated out of Olympic gold' after controversial final

Don't miss:BBC viewers fume 'we're fed up' as Olympics coverage becomes 'insufferable'

“As I was a total outsider, I only had one goal: to interact with the audience. I was almost there.”

Thankfully for Ammirati, he wasn’t the only pole vaulter to go viral over the weekend, with Estonian athlete Karel Tilga also enduring an afternoon to forget.

We use your sign-up to provide content in ways you've consented to and to improve our understanding of you. This may include adverts from us and 3rd parties based on our understanding. You can unsubscribe at any time. Read ourPrivacy Policy

Unlike Ammirati, the 26-year-old didn’t come close to clearing the bar, instead, his pole got stuck in the floor with Tilga left dangling precariously in the air.

Tilga eventually came back down to earth, but it wasn’t without a bang, as the Estonian crashed to the floor in a less-than-graceful heap.

title: Olympian who was denied a medal because of his penis told to 'call a drag queen' for help | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Amos Murphy
description: An Olympian had their manhood to blame for missing out on gold.
